questions.testName: December 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A linear model estimates the population of a city from 1996 to 2020. The model estimates the population was 61 thousand in 1996, 225 thousand in 2016, and $x$ thousand in 2020. To the nearest whole number, what is the value of $x$?
questions.questionText: A linear model estimates the population of a city from 1996 to 2020. The model estimates the population was 61 thousand in 1996, 225 thousand in 2016, and $x$ thousand in 2020. To the nearest whole number, what is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$y = 500(3)^{2x}$
The equation gives the estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six month period, $x$, starting from the end of January 1991, where $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the $y$-intercept of the graph of this equation in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText:
$y = 500(3)^{2x}$
The equation gives the estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six month period, $x$, starting from the end of January 1991, where $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the $y$-intercept of the graph of this equation in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six month period was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1991 was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1992 was 1,500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1991 was 1,500.

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$\frac{x}{2} + 9(y - 13) = 27$ and $\frac{x}{7} - 9(y - 13) = 45$
The solution to the given system of equations is $(x, y)$. What is the value of $9x$?
questions.questionText:
$\frac{x}{2} + 9(y - 13) = 27$ and $\frac{x}{7} - 9(y - 13) = 45$

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: When several resistors are connected in series in a circuit, the total resistance of the circuit is the sum of the resistances of these resistors. A certain circuit consists of 5 resistors connected in series, where the resistance of each resistor is positive. This circuit has a total resistance of 90 ohms. The total resistance of 2 resistors in this circuit is 60 ohms. Which inequality best represents all possible values of the resistance $x$, in ohms, of one of the other 3 resistors in this circuit?
questions.questionText: When several resistors are connected in series in a circuit, the total resistance of the circuit is the sum of the resistances of these resistors. A certain circuit consists of 5 resistors connected in series, where the resistance of each resistor is positive. This circuit has a total resistance of 90 ohms. The total resistance of 2 resistors in this circuit is 60 ohms. Which inequality best represents all possible values of the resistance $x$, in ohms, of one of the other 3 resistors in this circuit?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A family has money in an account for renting movies online. Each time the family rents a movie, the cost of the rental is withdrawn from the account, and each rental costs the same amount of money. The function $f(m) = 30 - 3m$ gives the amount of money, in dollars, in the account after the family has rented $m$ movies. Which of the following represents the amount of money, in dollars, withdrawn from the account each time the family rents a movie?
questions.questionText: A family has money in an account for renting movies online. Each time the family rents a movie, the cost of the rental is withdrawn from the account, and each rental costs the same amount of money. The function $f(m) = 30 - 3m$ gives the amount of money, in dollars, in the account after the family has rented $m$ movies. Which of the following represents the amount of money, in dollars, withdrawn from the account each time the family rents a movie?

If there are buildings on exactly $p\%$ of the square footage of the plot of land, what is the value of $p$?

questions.prompt: A rectangle has a length that is 49 times its width. The function $y = (49w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $49w$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length that is 49 times its width. The function $y = (49w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $49w$ in this context?

choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet
The area of the rectangle, in square feet
The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet
The width of the rectangle, in feet

choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six month period was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1991 was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1992 was 1,500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1991 was 1,500.
